'''諸陵寮'''(しょりょうりょう)は[[律令制]]において[[治部省]]に属する機関。
== 職掌・沿革 == 諸陵寮は[[律令]]では'''諸陵司'''として設置された。[[729年]]に[[寮 (律令制)|寮]]に昇格した。諸陵寮・諸陵司の職掌は[[陵墓]]の管理・喪葬・皇族葬儀の儀礼などである。特に陵墓管理については[[陵戸]]の名簿を管理してその守衛にあたらせた。凶事が起こった際には死者のたたりと考えて寮の役人を不遇であった皇族の陵墓に送ってこれを慰めた。[[早良親王]]に対するものが有名である。陵墓は現在､[[宮内庁書陵部]]が管理している。
== 職員 == *頭(従五位上) *助(正六位下) *大充(正七位下) 少充(従七位上) *大属(従八位上) 大属(従八位下)
*史生
*使部 *直丁
*陵戸 *守戸
(諸陵司の職員) *正（正六位上） *佑（従七位下） *大令史（大初位上） *少令史（大初位下）
*土部 葬儀の際に儀礼を執り行う伴部
*使部 *直丁
*陵戸 *守戸
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の官制]] *[[喪儀司]]
{{Japanese-hisory-stub|しよりようりよう}} [[category:律令制の官制|しよりようりよう]]

revision.comment: /*明治政府*/細かい改正の沿革。神祇官諸陵寮->神祇省->教部省諸陵課/諸陵掛->宮内省御陵墓掛/御陵墓課/諸陵寮 の経路。
revision.text:
'''諸陵寮'''(しょりょうりょう)は[[律令制]]において[[治部省]]に属した機関。明治政府でも復活している。陵墓は現在､[[宮内庁書陵部]]が管理している。
== 律令制での職掌・沿革 == 諸陵寮は[[律令]]では'''諸陵司'''（和訓：みささぎのつかさ）として設置された。[[729年]]に[[寮 (律令制)|寮]]に昇格した。諸陵寮・諸陵司の職掌は[[陵墓]]の管理・[[喪葬]]・[[皇族]][[葬儀]]の儀礼などである。特に陵墓管理については[[陵戸]]の名簿を管理してその守衛にあたらせた。凶事が起こった際には死者のたたりと考えて寮の役人を不遇であった[[皇族]]の陵墓に送ってこれを慰めた。[[早良親王]]に対するものが有名である。
== 職員 == * 頭([[従五位|従五位上]]) * 助([[正六位|正六位下]]) * 大允([[従六位#正七位|正七位下]]) 少允（[[従六位#従七位|従七位上]]) * 大属([[従六位#従八位|従八位上]]) 大属([[従六位#従八位|従八位下]])
* 史生
* 使部 * 直丁
* 陵戸 * 守戸
(諸陵司の職員) * 正（[[正六位|正六位上]]） * 佑（[[従六位#従七位|従七位下]]） * 大令史（[[従六位#大初位|大初位上]]） * 少令史（[[従六位#大初位|大初位下]]）
* 土部 [[葬儀]]の際に儀礼を執り行う伴部
* 使部 * 直丁
* 陵戸 * 守戸
==明治政府== 明治政府では、[[1869年]]([[明治2年]]9月17日)公布において諸陵寮は再興されて、[[神祇官]]の配属とし、頭、助、允、属等の官員を置いたが、このとき諸陵頭は[[宮内大丞]]の[[戸田忠至]]が兼任した。<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|location=第4章祭政一致|pages=478-|url=http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1242382/260|id={{近代デジタルライブラリー|1242382}}}}</ref><ref name=toike>{{cite book|和書|last=外池|first=昇|authorlink=外池昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2000|origyear=1999|page= 71|format=snippet|url=books.google.co.jp/books?id=c7cyAQAAIAAJ|isbn=4-642-05483-9}} 13-ISBN 978-4-642-05483-6 </ref>。このとき歴代の陵墓の調査などがおこなわれ、[[1870年]]([[明治3年]]7月23日)大友皇子に[[弘文天皇]]、大炊王に[[淳仁天皇]]、懐成親王に[[仲恭天皇]]の[[諡号]]が追贈された<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。
[[1871年]](明治4年8月4日)、[[神祇省]]に統廃合されたが、神祇省を廃止分割して1872年(明治5年3月14日)に新設した[[教部省]]に諸陵課が置かれた<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。教部省の1874年(明治7年8月3日)に諸陵掛を設置<ref name=toike/>。のちに事務が[[宮内省]]に移管され、[[1878年]](明治11年3月1日)に宮内省[[御陵墓掛]]が置かれ<ref name=toike/>、[[1883年]](明治16 年 1月19日に御陵墓課に改められ、ついに[[1888年]](明治19年2月4日)諸陵寮が復興<ref name=toike/><ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。
==出典== {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[日本の官制]] * [[喪儀司]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しよりようりよう}} [[Category:律令制の官制]] [[Category:令外官]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.comment: <ref> linkfix; 明治19=1886年(訂正)。序文を多少改稿。
revision.text:
'''諸陵寮'''(しょりょうりょう)は[[律令制]]において[[治部省]]に属した機関。明治政府初年にも諸陵寮は短い年間復活し廃止されたが、再編改称などを経て、1886年再興をはたしている。陵墓は現在､その後身である[[宮内庁書陵部]]が管理している。
== 律令制での職掌・沿革 == 諸陵寮は[[律令]]では'''諸陵司'''（和訓：みささぎのつかさ）として設置された。[[729年]]に[[寮 (律令制)|寮]]に昇格した。諸陵寮・諸陵司の職掌は[[陵墓]]の管理・[[喪葬]]・[[皇族]][[葬儀]]の儀礼などである。特に陵墓管理については[[陵戸]]の名簿を管理してその守衛にあたらせた。凶事が起こった際には死者のたたりと考えて寮の役人を不遇であった[[皇族]]の陵墓に送ってこれを慰めた。[[早良親王]]に対するものが有名である。
== 職員 == * 頭([[従五位|従五位上]]) * 助([[正六位|正六位下]]) * 大允([[従六位#正七位|正七位下]]) 少允（[[従六位#従七位|従七位上]]) * 大属([[従六位#従八位|従八位上]]) 大属([[従六位#従八位|従八位下]])
* 史生
* 使部 * 直丁
* 陵戸 * 守戸
(諸陵司の職員) * 正（[[正六位|正六位上]]） * 佑（[[従六位#従七位|従七位下]]） * 大令史（[[従六位#大初位|大初位上]]） * 少令史（[[従六位#大初位|大初位下]]）
* 土部 [[葬儀]]の際に儀礼を執り行う伴部
* 使部 * 直丁
* 陵戸 * 守戸
==明治政府== 明治政府では、[[1869年]]([[明治2年]]9月17日)公布において諸陵寮は再興されて、[[神祇官]]の配属とし、頭、助、允、属等の官員を置いたが、このとき諸陵頭は[[宮内大丞]]の[[戸田忠至]]が兼任した。<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|location=第4章祭政一致|pages=478-|url=http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1242382/260|id={{近代デジタルライブラリー|1242382}}}}</ref><ref name=toike>{{cite book|和書|last=外池|first=昇|authorlink=外池昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2000|origyear=1999|page= 71|format=snippet|url=//books.google.co.jp/books?id=c7cyAQAAIAAJ|isbn=4-642-05483-9}} 13-ISBN 978-4-642-05483-6 </ref>。このとき歴代の陵墓の調査などがおこなわれ、[[1870年]]([[明治3年]]7月23日)大友皇子に[[弘文天皇]]、大炊王に[[淳仁天皇]]、懐成親王に[[仲恭天皇]]の[[諡号]]が追贈された<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。
[[1871年]](明治4年8月4日)、[[神祇省]]に統廃合されたが、神祇省を廃止分割して1872年(明治5年3月14日)に新設した[[教部省]]に諸陵課が置かれた<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。教部省の1874年(明治7年8月3日)に諸陵掛を設置<ref name=toike/>。のちに事務が[[宮内省]]に移管され、[[1878年]](明治11年3月1日)に宮内省[[御陵墓掛]]が置かれ<ref name=toike/>、[[1883年]](明治16 年 1月19日に御陵墓課に改められ、ついに[[1886年]](明治19年2月4日)諸陵寮が復興<ref name=toike/><ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。
==出典== {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[日本の官制]] * [[喪儀司]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しよりようりよう}} [[Category:律令制の官制]] [[Category:令外官]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''諸陵寮'''(しょりょうりょう)は[[律令制]]において[[治部省]]に属した機関。明治政府初年にも諸陵寮は短い年間復活しいちど廃止されたが、再編改称などを経て、1886年再興をはたしている。その後身である[[宮内庁書陵部]]が現在、陵墓（[[天皇陵]]）を管理している。
== 律令制での職掌・沿革 == 諸陵寮は[[律令]]では'''諸陵司'''（和訓：みささぎのつかさ）として設置された。[[729年]]に[[寮 (律令制)|寮]]に昇格した。諸陵寮・諸陵司の職掌は[[陵墓]]の管理・[[喪葬]]・[[皇族]][[葬儀]]の儀礼などである。特に陵墓管理については[[陵戸]]の名簿を管理してその守衛にあたらせた。凶事が起こった際には死者のたたりと考えて寮の役人を不遇であった[[皇族]]の陵墓に送ってこれを慰めた。[[早良親王]]に対するものが有名である。
== 職員 == * 頭([[従五位|従五位上]]) * 助([[正六位|正六位下]]) * 大允([[従六位#正七位|正七位下]]) 少允（[[従六位#従七位|従七位上]]) * 大属([[従六位#従八位|従八位上]]) 大属([[従六位#従八位|従八位下]])
* 史生
* 使部 * 直丁
* 陵戸 * 守戸
(諸陵司の職員) * 正（[[正六位|正六位上]]） * 佑（[[従六位#従七位|従七位下]]） * 大令史（[[従六位#大初位|大初位上]]） * 少令史（[[従六位#大初位|大初位下]]）
* 土部 [[葬儀]]の際に儀礼を執り行う伴部
* 使部 * 直丁
* 陵戸 * 守戸
==明治政府== 明治政府では、[[1869年]]([[明治2年]]9月17日)公布において諸陵寮は再興されて、[[神祇官]]の配属とし、頭、助、允、属等の官員を置いたが、このとき諸陵頭は[[宮内大丞]]の[[戸田忠至]]が兼任した。<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|location=第4章祭政一致|pages=478-|url=http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1242382/260|id={{近代デジタルライブラリー|1242382}}}}</ref><ref name=toike>{{cite book|和書|last=外池|first=昇|authorlink=外池昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2000|origyear=1999|page= 71|format=snippet|url=//books.google.co.jp/books?id=c7cyAQAAIAAJ|isbn=4-642-05483-9}} 13-ISBN 978-4-642-05483-6 </ref>。このとき歴代の陵墓の調査などがおこなわれ、[[1870年]]([[明治3年]]7月23日)大友皇子に[[弘文天皇]]、大炊王に[[淳仁天皇]]、懐成親王に[[仲恭天皇]]の[[諡号]]が追贈された<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。
[[1871年]](明治4年8月4日)、諸陵寮は[[神祇省]]に統廃合されたが、神祇省を廃止分割して1872年(明治5年3月14日)に新設した[[教部省]]に諸陵課が置かれた<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。教部省は、1874年(明治7年8月3日)に諸陵掛を設置<ref name=toike/>。のちに事務が[[宮内省]]に移管され、[[1878年]](明治11年3月1日)に宮内省御陵墓掛が置かれ<ref name=toike/>、[[1883年]](明治16 年 1月19日)に御陵墓課に改められ、ついに[[1886年]](明治19年2月4日)諸陵寮が復興した<ref name=toike/><ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。
==出典== {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[日本の官制]] * [[喪儀司]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しよりようりよう}} [[Category:律令制の官制]] [[Category:令外官]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''諸陵寮'''(みささぎのつかさ)は[[律令制]]において[[治部省]]に属した機関。明治政府初年にも諸陵寮は短い年間復活しいちど廃止されたが、再編改称などを経て、1886年再興をはたしている。その後身である[[宮内庁書陵部]]が現在、陵墓（[[天皇陵]]）を管理している。
== 律令制での職掌・沿革 == 諸陵寮は[[律令]]では'''諸陵司'''（和訓：みささぎのつかさ）として設置された。[[729年]]に[[寮 (律令制)|寮]]に昇格した。諸陵寮・諸陵司の職掌は[[陵墓]]の管理・[[喪葬]]・[[皇族]][[葬儀]]の儀礼などである。特に陵墓管理については[[陵戸]]の名簿を管理してその守衛にあたらせた。凶事が起こった際には死者のたたりと考えて寮の役人を不遇であった[[皇族]]の陵墓に送ってこれを慰めた。[[早良親王]]に対するものが有名である。
== 職員 == * 頭([[従五位|従五位上]]) * 助([[正六位|正六位下]]) * 大允([[正七位|正七位下]]) 少允（[[従七位|従七位上]]) * 大属([[従八位|従八位上]]) 大属([[従八位|従八位下]])
* 史生
* 使部 * 直丁
* 陵戸 * 守戸
(諸陵司の職員) * 正（[[正六位|正六位上]]） * 佑（[[従七位|従七位下]]） * 大令史（[[大初位|大初位上]]） * 少令史（[[大初位|大初位下]]）
* 土部 [[葬儀]]の際に儀礼を執り行う伴部
* 使部 * 直丁
* 陵戸 * 守戸
==明治政府== 明治政府では、[[1869年]]([[明治2年]]9月17日)公布において諸陵寮は再興されて、[[神祇官]]の配属とし、頭、助、允、属等の官員を置いたが、このとき諸陵頭は[[宮内大丞]]の[[戸田忠至]]が兼任した。<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|location=第4章祭政一致|pages=478-|url={{NDLDC|1242382/260}}|id={{近代デジタルライブラリー|1242382}}}}</ref><ref name="toike">{{cite book|和書|last=外池|first=昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|url=//books.google.co.jp/books?id=c7cyAQAAIAAJ|format=snippet|year=2000|publisher=吉川弘文館|page=71|authorlink=外池昇|origyear=1999|isbn=4-642-05483-9}} ISBN 978-4-642-05483-6 </ref>。このとき歴代の陵墓の調査などがおこなわれ、[[1870年]]([[明治3年]]7月23日)大友皇子に[[弘文天皇]]、大炊王に[[淳仁天皇]]、懐成親王に[[仲恭天皇]]の[[諡号]]が追贈された<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。
[[1871年]](明治4年8月4日)、諸陵寮は[[神祇省]]に統廃合されたが、神祇省を廃止分割して1872年(明治5年3月14日)に新設した[[教部省]]に諸陵課が置かれた<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。教部省は、1874年(明治7年8月3日)に諸陵掛を設置<ref name=toike/>。のちに事務が[[宮内省]]に移管され、[[1878年]](明治11年3月1日)に宮内省御陵墓掛が置かれ<ref name=toike/>、[[1883年]](明治16 年 1月19日)に御陵墓課に改められ、ついに[[1886年]](明治19年2月4日)諸陵寮が復興した<ref name=toike/><ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。
==出典== {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[日本の官制]] * [[喪儀司]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しよりようりよう}} [[Category:律令制の官制]] [[Category:令外官]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.comment: 読み修正：『国史大辞典』等の主要事典類に従う。
revision.text:
'''諸陵寮'''（しょりょうりょう/みささぎのつかさ）は、[[律令制]]において[[治部省]]に属した機関。明治政府初年にも諸陵寮は短い年間復活しいちど廃止されたが、再編改称などを経て、1886年再興をはたしている。その後身である[[宮内庁書陵部]]が現在、陵墓（[[天皇陵]]）を管理している。
== 律令制での職掌・沿革 == 諸陵寮は[[律令]]では'''諸陵司'''（和訓：みささぎのつかさ）として設置された。[[729年]]に[[寮 (律令制)|寮]]に昇格した。諸陵寮・諸陵司の職掌は[[陵墓]]の管理・[[喪葬]]・[[皇族]][[葬儀]]の儀礼などである。特に陵墓管理については[[陵戸]]の名簿を管理してその守衛にあたらせた。凶事が起こった際には死者のたたりと考えて寮の役人を不遇であった[[皇族]]の陵墓に送ってこれを慰めた。[[早良親王]]に対するものが有名である。
== 職員 == * 頭([[従五位|従五位上]]) * 助([[正六位|正六位下]]) * 大允([[正七位|正七位下]]) 少允（[[従七位|従七位上]]) * 大属([[従八位|従八位上]]) 大属([[従八位|従八位下]])
* 史生
* 使部 * 直丁
* 陵戸 * 守戸
(諸陵司の職員) * 正（[[正六位|正六位上]]） * 佑（[[従七位|従七位下]]） * 大令史（[[大初位|大初位上]]） * 少令史（[[大初位|大初位下]]）
* 土部 [[葬儀]]の際に儀礼を執り行う伴部
* 使部 * 直丁
* 陵戸 * 守戸
==明治政府== 明治政府では、[[1869年]]([[明治2年]]9月17日)公布において諸陵寮は再興されて、[[神祇官]]の配属とし、頭、助、允、属等の官員を置いたが、このとき諸陵頭は[[宮内大丞]]の[[戸田忠至]]が兼任した。<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|location=第4章祭政一致|pages=478-|url={{NDLDC|1242382/260}}|id={{近代デジタルライブラリー|1242382}}}}</ref><ref name="toike">{{cite book|和書|last=外池|first=昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|url=//books.google.co.jp/books?id=c7cyAQAAIAAJ|format=snippet|year=2000|publisher=吉川弘文館|page=71|authorlink=外池昇|origyear=1999|isbn=4-642-05483-9}} ISBN 978-4-642-05483-6 </ref>。このとき歴代の陵墓の調査などがおこなわれ、[[1870年]]([[明治3年]]7月23日)大友皇子に[[弘文天皇]]、大炊王に[[淳仁天皇]]、懐成親王に[[仲恭天皇]]の[[諡号]]が追贈された<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。
[[1871年]](明治4年8月4日)、諸陵寮は[[神祇省]]に統廃合されたが、神祇省を廃止分割して1872年(明治5年3月14日)に新設した[[教部省]]に諸陵課が置かれた<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。教部省は、1874年(明治7年8月3日)に諸陵掛を設置<ref name=toike/>。のちに事務が[[宮内省]]に移管され、[[1878年]](明治11年3月1日)に宮内省御陵墓掛が置かれ<ref name=toike/>、[[1883年]](明治16 年 1月19日)に御陵墓課に改められ、ついに[[1886年]](明治19年2月4日)諸陵寮が復興した<ref name=toike/><ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。
==出典== {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[日本の官制]] * [[喪儀司]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しよりようりよう}} [[Category:律令制の官制]] [[Category:令外官]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{宮内省}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しよりようりよう}} [[Category:律令制の官制]] [[Category:令外官]] [[Category:宮内省]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''諸陵寮'''（しょりょうりょう/みささぎのつかさ）は、[[律令制]]において[[治部省]]に属した機関。明治政府初年にも諸陵寮は短い年間復活しいちど廃止されたが、再編改称などを経て、1886年再興をはたしている。その後身である[[宮内庁書陵部]]が現在、陵墓（[[天皇陵]]）を管理している。
== 律令制での職掌・沿革 == 諸陵寮は[[律令]]では'''諸陵司'''（和訓：みささぎのつかさ）として設置された。[[729年]]に[[寮 (律令制)|寮]]に昇格した。諸陵寮・諸陵司の職掌は[[陵墓]]の管理・[[喪葬]]・[[皇族]][[葬儀]]の儀礼などである。特に陵墓管理については[[陵戸]]の名簿を管理してその守衛にあたらせた。凶事が起こった際には死者のたたりと考えて寮の役人を不遇であった[[皇族]]の陵墓に送ってこれを慰めた。[[早良親王]]に対するものが有名である。
== 職員 == * 頭([[従五位|従五位上]]) * 助([[正六位|正六位下]]) * 大允([[正七位|正七位下]]) 少允（[[従七位|従七位上]]) * 大属([[従八位|従八位上]]) 大属([[従八位|従八位下]])
* 史生
* 使部 * 直丁
* 陵戸 * 守戸
(諸陵司の職員) * 正（[[正六位|正六位上]]） * 佑（[[従七位|従七位下]]） * 大令史（[[大初位|大初位上]]） * 少令史（[[大初位|大初位下]]）
* 土部 [[葬儀]]の際に儀礼を執り行う伴部
* 使部 * 直丁
* 陵戸 * 守戸
==明治政府== 明治政府では、[[1869年]]([[明治2年]]9月17日)公布において諸陵寮は再興されて、[[神祇官]]の配属とし、頭、助、允、属等の官員を置いたが、このとき諸陵頭は[[宮内大丞]]の[[戸田忠至]]が兼任した。<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|location=第4章祭政一致|pages=478-|url={{NDLDC|1242382/260}}|id={{近代デジタルライブラリー|1242382}}}}</ref><ref name="toike">{{cite book|和書|last=外池|first=昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|url=//books.google.co.jp/books?id=c7cyAQAAIAAJ|format=snippet|year=2000|publisher=吉川弘文館|page=71|authorlink=外池昇|origyear=1999|isbn=4-642-05483-9}} ISBN 978-4-642-05483-6 </ref>。このとき歴代の陵墓の調査などがおこなわれ、[[1870年]]([[明治3年]]7月23日)大友皇子に[[弘文天皇]]、大炊王に[[淳仁天皇]]、懐成親王に[[仲恭天皇]]の[[諡号]]が追贈された<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。
[[1871年]](明治4年8月4日)、諸陵寮は[[神祇省]]に統廃合されたが、神祇省を廃止分割して1872年(明治5年3月14日)に新設した[[教部省]]に諸陵課が置かれた<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。教部省は、1874年(明治7年8月3日)に諸陵掛を設置<ref name=toike/>。のちに事務が[[宮内省]]に移管され、[[1878年]](明治11年3月1日)に宮内省御陵墓掛が置かれ<ref name=toike/>、[[1883年]](明治16 年 1月19日)に御陵墓課に改められ、ついに[[1886年]](明治19年2月4日)諸陵寮が復興した<ref name=toike/><ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。
==出典== {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[日本の官制]] * [[諸陵式]] * [[喪儀司]]
{{宮内省}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しよりようりよう}} [[Category:律令制の官制]] [[Category:令外官]] [[Category:宮内省]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''諸陵寮'''（しょりょうりょう/みささぎのつかさ）は、[[律令制]]において[[治部省]]に属した機関。明治政府初年にも諸陵寮は短い年間復活し一度廃止されたが、再編改称などを経て、[[1886年]]（[[明治]]19年）再興をはたしている。その後身である[[宮内庁書陵部]]が現在、陵墓（[[天皇陵]]）を管理している。
== 律令制での職掌・沿革 == 諸陵寮は[[律令]]では'''諸陵司'''（和訓：みささぎのつかさ）として設置された。[[729年]]（[[神亀]]6年/[[天平]]元年）に[[寮 (律令制)|寮]]に昇格した。諸陵寮・諸陵司の職掌は[[陵墓]]の管理・[[喪葬]]・[[皇族]][[葬儀]]の儀礼などである。特に陵墓管理については[[陵戸]]の名簿を管理してその守衛にあたらせた。凶事が起こった際には死者のたたりと考えて寮の役人を不遇であった[[皇族]]の陵墓に送ってこれを慰めた。[[早良親王]]に対するものが有名である。
== 職員 == * 頭([[従五位|従五位上]]) * 助([[正六位|正六位下]]) * 大允([[正七位|正七位下]]) 少允（[[従七位|従七位上]]) * 大属([[従八位|従八位上]]) 大属([[従八位|従八位下]])
* 史生
* 使部 * 直丁
* 陵戸 * 守戸
(諸陵司の職員) * 正（[[正六位|正六位上]]） * 佑（[[従七位|従七位下]]） * 大令史（[[大初位|大初位上]]） * 少令史（[[大初位|大初位下]]）
* 土部 [[葬儀]]の際に儀礼を執り行う伴部
* 使部 * 直丁
* 陵戸 * 守戸
== 明治政府 == 明治政府では、[[1869年]]（[[明治2年]]9月17日）公布において諸陵寮は再興されて、[[神祇官]]の配属とし、頭、助、允、属等の官員を置いたが、このとき諸陵頭は[[宮内省|宮内大丞]]の[[戸田忠至]]が兼任した。<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|location=第4章祭政一致|pages=478-|url={{NDLDC|1242382/260}}|id={{近代デジタルライブラリー|1242382}}}}</ref><ref name="toike">{{cite book|和書|last=外池|first=昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|url=//books.google.co.jp/books?id=c7cyAQAAIAAJ|format=snippet|year=2000|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|page=71|authorlink=外池昇|origyear=1999|isbn=4-642-05483-9}} ISBN 978-4-642-05483-6 </ref>。このとき歴代の陵墓の調査などがおこなわれ、[[1870年]]([[明治3年]])7月23日、大友皇子に[[弘文天皇]]、大炊王に[[淳仁天皇]]、懐成親王に[[仲恭天皇]]の[[諡号]]が[[追贈]]された<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。
[[1871年]]（[[明治4年]]）8月4日、諸陵寮は[[神祇省]]に統廃合されたが、神祇省を廃止分割して[[1872年]]（[[明治5年]]）3月14日に新設した[[教部省]]に諸陵課が置かれた<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。教部省は、[[1874年]]（[[明治]]7年）8月3日に諸陵掛を設置<ref name=toike/>。のちに事務が[[宮内省]]に移管され、[[1878年]]（明治11年）3月1日に宮内省御陵墓掛が置かれ<ref name=toike/>、[[1883年]]（明治16年）1月19日に御陵墓課に改められ、ついに[[1886年]]（明治19年）2月4日、諸陵寮が復興した<ref name=toike/><ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。
== 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の官制]] * [[諸陵式]] * [[喪儀司]]
{{宮内省}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.comment: Created page with "'''Shoryoryo''' (諸陵寮) was a {{ill|government office|ja|律令制}} under the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs|ja|治部省}} during the {{ill|Ritsuryo system|ja|律令制}} in Japan. It was briefly revived in the early years of the Meiji government, abolished once, and later re-established with modifications in {{ill|1886|ja|1886年}}. * 陵戸 * 守戸
== 明治政府 == 明治政府では、[[1869年]]（[[明治2年]]9月17日）公布において諸陵寮は再興されて、[[神祇官]]の配属とし、頭、助、允、属等の官員を置いたが、このとき諸陵頭は[[宮内省|宮内大丞]]の[[戸田忠至]]が兼任した。<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|location=第4章祭政一致|pages=478-|url={{NDLDC|1242382/260}}|id={{近代デジタルライブラリー|1242382}}}}</ref><ref name="toike">{{cite book|和書|last=外池|first=昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|url=//books.google.co.jp/books?id=c7cyAQAAIAAJ|format=snippet|year=2000|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|page=71|authorlink=外池昇|origyear=1999|isbn=4-642-05483-9}} ISBN 978-4-642-05483-6 </ref>。このとき歴代の陵墓の調査などがおこなわれ、[[1870年]]([[明治3年]])7月23日、大友皇子に[[弘文天皇]]、大炊王に[[淳仁天皇]]、懐成親王に[[仲恭天皇]]の[[諡号]]が[[追贈]]された<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。
[[1871年]]（[[明治4年]]）8月4日、諸陵寮は[[神祇省]]に統廃合されたが、神祇省を廃止分割して[[1872年]]（[[明治5年]]）3月14日に新設した[[教部省]]に諸陵課が置かれた<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。教部省は、[[1874年]]（[[明治]]7年）8月3日に諸陵掛を設置<ref name=toike/>。のちに事務が[[宮内省]]に移管され、[[1878年]]（明治11年）3月1日に宮内省御陵墓掛が置かれ<ref name=toike/>、[[1883年]]（明治16年）1月19日に御陵墓課に改められ、ついに[[1886年]]（明治19年）2月4日、諸陵寮が復興した<ref name=toike/><ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。
== 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の官制]] * [[諸陵式]] * [[喪儀司]]
{{宮内省}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[諸陵寮]] to [[Shoryoryo]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
'''諸陵寮'''（しょりょうりょう/みささぎのつかさ）は、[[律令制]]において[[治部省]]に属した機関。明治政府初年にも諸陵寮は短い年間復活し一度廃止されたが、再編改称などを経て、[[1886年]]（[[明治]]19年）再興をはたしている。その後身である[[宮内庁書陵部]]が現在、陵墓（[[天皇陵]]）を管理している。
== 律令制での職掌・沿革 == 諸陵寮は[[律令]]では'''諸陵司'''（和訓：みささぎのつかさ）として設置された。[[729年]]（[[神亀]]6年/[[天平]]元年）に[[寮 (律令制)|寮]]に昇格した。諸陵寮・諸陵司の職掌は[[陵墓]]の管理・[[喪葬]]・[[皇族]][[葬儀]]の儀礼などである。特に陵墓管理については[[陵戸]]の名簿を管理してその守衛にあたらせた。凶事が起こった際には死者のたたりと考えて寮の役人を不遇であった[[皇族]]の陵墓に送ってこれを慰めた。[[早良親王]]に対するものが有名である。
== 職員 == * 頭([[従五位|従五位上]]) * 助([[正六位|正六位下]]) * 大允([[正七位|正七位下]]) 少允（[[従七位|従七位上]]) * 大属([[従八位|従八位上]]) 大属([[従八位|従八位下]])
* 史生
* 使部 * 直丁
* 陵戸 * 守戸
(諸陵司の職員) * 正（[[正六位|正六位上]]） * 佑（[[従七位|従七位下]]） * 大令史（[[大初位|大初位上]]） * 少令史（[[大初位|大初位下]]）
* 土部 [[葬儀]]の際に儀礼を執り行う伴部
* 使部 * 直丁
* 陵戸 * 守戸
== 明治政府 == 明治政府では、[[1869年]]（[[明治2年]]9月17日）公布において諸陵寮は再興されて、[[神祇官]]の配属とし、頭、助、允、属等の官員を置いたが、このとき諸陵頭は[[宮内省|宮内大丞]]の[[戸田忠至]]が兼任した。<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|location=第4章祭政一致|pages=478-|url={{NDLDC|1242382/260}}|id={{近代デジタルライブラリー|1242382}}}}</ref><ref name="toike">{{cite book|和書|last=外池|first=昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|url=//books.google.co.jp/books?id=c7cyAQAAIAAJ|format=snippet|year=2000|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|page=71|authorlink=外池昇|origyear=1999|isbn=4-642-05483-9}} ISBN 978-4-642-05483-6 </ref>。このとき歴代の陵墓の調査などがおこなわれ、[[1870年]]([[明治3年]])7月23日、大友皇子に[[弘文天皇]]、大炊王に[[淳仁天皇]]、懐成親王に[[仲恭天皇]]の[[諡号]]が[[追贈]]された<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。
[[1871年]]（[[明治4年]]）8月4日、諸陵寮は[[神祇省]]に統廃合されたが、神祇省を廃止分割して[[1872年]]（[[明治5年]]）3月14日に新設した[[教部省]]に諸陵課が置かれた<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。教部省は、[[1874年]]（[[明治]]7年）8月3日に諸陵掛を設置<ref name=toike/>。のちに事務が[[宮内省]]に移管され、[[1878年]]（明治11年）3月1日に宮内省御陵墓掛が置かれ<ref name=toike/>、[[1883年]]（明治16年）1月19日に御陵墓課に改められ、ついに[[1886年]]（明治19年）2月4日、諸陵寮が復興した<ref name=toike/><ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>。
== 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の官制]] * [[諸陵式]] * [[喪儀司]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諸陵寮]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11632153]]
Wikidata name: Shoryō-ryō
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Shoryō-ryō''' (諸陵寮) was a [[:en:Ritsuryō|government office]] under the [[:en:Ministry of Ceremonies (Japan)|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] during the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]] in Japan. It was briefly revived in the early years of the Meiji government, abolished once, and later re-established with modifications in [[:en:1886|1886]]. Its successor, the [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], currently manages imperial tombs ([[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]]).

== Duties and History under the Ritsuryo System ==
The Shoryō-ryō was initially established as {{ill|Shoryoshi|ja|諸陵司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|1=Shoryō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632153}} (和訓: みささぎのつかさ) under the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryo Code]]. In [[:en:729|729]] ([[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] 6/ [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyo]] 元年), it was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Dormitory|ryo]]. Its primary duties included managing {{ill|Imperial tombs|ja|陵墓|1=Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial tombs|qq=}}, conducting {{ill|funeral rites|ja|喪葬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/funeral rites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and overseeing rituals for the funerals of members of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|qq=|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Specifically, it managed the registries of the {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|qq=|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}, who were assigned to guard the tombs. In cases of misfortune, officials were sometimes sent to console the spirits of neglected Imperial Family members, as seen in the case of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]].

== Staff ==
* [[:en:Head|Head (従五位上)]]
* {{ill|Assistant (正六位下)|ja|助|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant (正六位下)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Junior Assistant (正七位下)|ja|允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant (正七位下)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|1=ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457645}}
* {{ill|Clerk (従七位上)|ja|属|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clerk (従七位上)|qq=|1=Clerk (従七位上)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124394399}}

* {{ill|Shisho|ja|史生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho|qq=|1=shishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412126}}
* {{ill|Shibu|ja|使部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Chokutei|ja|直丁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokutei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

* {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|qq=|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}
* {{ill|Shugo|ja|守戸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夙|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夙|1=shuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430570}}

=== Staff of Shoryoshi ===
* {{ill|Chief (正六位上)|ja|正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief (正六位上)|qq=|1=正|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3594991}}
* {{ill|Deputy (従七位下)|ja|佑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deputy (従七位下)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Senior Clerk (大初位上)|ja|大令史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Clerk (大初位上)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Junior Clerk (大初位下)|ja|少令史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Clerk (大初位下)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* [[:en:Radical 32|Tsubebe]] – Ritual staff for funerals.

== Meiji Government ==
In [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (Meiji 2), the Shoryō-ryō was re-established under the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] with roles such as head, assistant, clerk, and others. The head of the Shoryō-ryō at the time was {{ill|Toda Tadazumi|ja|戸田忠至|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadazumi|qq=|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}}, who also served as a {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja|宮内大丞|Ministry of the Imperial Household=Ministry of the Imperial Household|lt=Miyauchi-daiyoku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193428}}.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|pages=478|url={{NDLDC|1242382/260}}}}</ref><ref name="toike">{{cite book|和書|author=外池昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2000|page=71}}</ref> During this time, the tombs of past emperors were re-evaluated, and posthumous titles were granted, such as [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Emperor Kobun]] and [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>

In [[:en:1871|Meiji 4]], the Shoryō-ryō was merged into the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Divinities]]. After several reorganizations, it was incorporated into the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] as the Shoryo Section. Later, the management was transferred to the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]], where it became the Imperial Tombs Section in 1878.<ref name=toike/>

In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]], the Shoryō-ryō was revived.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/><ref name=toike/>

== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Government of Japan|ja|日本の官制|1=structure of the ritsuryō government|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese administrative system|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193883}}
* {{ill|Shoryoshiki|ja|諸陵式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryoshiki|qq=|1=Shoryoshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632154}}
* {{ill|Mogishi|ja|喪儀司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogishi|qq=|1=Mogishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419293}}

{{Japanese-history-stub}}          

{{translated page|ja|諸陵寮|version=88364051|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Meiji Government ==
In [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (Meiji 2), the Shoryō-ryō was re-established under the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] with roles such as head, assistant, clerk, and others. The head of the Shoryō-ryō at the time was {{ill|Toda Tadazumi|ja|戸田忠至|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadazumi|qq=|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}}, who also served as a {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja|宮内大丞|Ministry of the Imperial Household=Ministry of the Imperial Household|lt=Miyauchi-daiyoku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193428}}.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|pages=478|url={{NDLDC|1242382/260}}}}</ref><ref name="toike">{{cite book|和書|author=外池昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2000|page=71}}</ref> During this time, the tombs of past emperors were re-evaluated, and posthumous titles were granted, such as [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Emperor Kobun]] and [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>

In [[:en:1871|Meiji 4]], the Shoryō-ryō was merged into the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Divinities]]. After several reorganizations, it was incorporated into the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] as the Shoryo Section. Later, the management was transferred to the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]], where it became the Imperial Tombs Section in 1878.<ref name=toike/>

In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]], the Shoryō-ryō was revived.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/><ref name=toike/>

Wikidata name: Shoryō-ryō
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Shoryō-ryō''' (諸陵寮) was a [[:en:Ritsuryō|government office]] under the [[:en:Ministry of Ceremonies (Japan)|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] during the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]] in Japan. It was briefly revived in the early years of the Meiji government, abolished once, and later re-established with modifications in [[:en:1886|1886]]. Its successor, the [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], currently manages imperial tombs ([[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]]).

== Duties and History under the Ritsuryo System ==
The Shoryō-ryō was initially established as {{ill|Shoryoshi|ja|諸陵司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|1=Shoryō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632153}} (和訓: みささぎのつかさ) under the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryo Code]]. In [[:en:729|729]] ([[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] 6/ [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyo]] 元年), it was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Dormitory|ryo]]. Its primary duties included managing {{ill|Imperial tombs|ja|陵墓|1=Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial tombs|qq=}}, conducting {{ill|funeral rites|ja|喪葬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/funeral rites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and overseeing rituals for the funerals of members of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Specifically, it managed the registries of the {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}, who were assigned to guard the tombs. In cases of misfortune, officials were sometimes sent to console the spirits of neglected Imperial Family members, as seen in the case of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]].

* {{ill|Shisho|ja|史生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho|1=shishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412126}}

In [[:en:1871|Meiji 4]], the Shoryō-ryō was merged into the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Divinities]]. After several reorganizations, it was incorporated into the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] as the Shoryo Section. Later, the management was transferred to the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]], where it became the Imperial Tombs Section in 1878.<ref name=toike/>

=== Staff of Shoryoshi ===

* {{ill|Deputy (従七位下)|ja|佑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deputy (従七位下)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

* [[:en:Radical 32|Tsubebe]] – Ritual staff for funerals.

== Meiji Government ==
In [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (Meiji 2), the Shoryō-ryō was re-established under the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] with roles such as head, assistant, clerk, and others. The head of the Shoryō-ryō at the time was {{ill|Toda Tadazumi|ja|戸田忠至|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadazumi|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}}, who also served as a {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja|宮内大丞|Ministry of the Imperial Household=Ministry of the Imperial Household|lt=Miyauchi-daiyoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193428}}.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|pages=478|url={{NDLDC|1242382/260}}}}</ref><ref name="toike">{{cite book|和書|author=外池昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2000|page=71}}</ref> During this time, the tombs of past emperors were re-evaluated, and posthumous titles were granted, such as [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Emperor Kobun]] and [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>

In [[:en:1871|Meiji 4]], the Shoryō-ryō was merged into the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Divinities]]. After several reorganizations, it was incorporated into the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] as the Shoryo Section. Later, the management was transferred to the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]], where it became the Imperial Tombs Section in 1878.<ref name=toike/>

In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]], the Shoryō-ryō was revived.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/><ref name=toike/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諸陵寮]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11632153]]
Wikidata name: Shoryō-ryō
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Shoryō-ryō''' (諸陵寮) was a [[:en:Ritsuryō|government office]] under the [[:en:Ministry of Ceremonies (Japan)|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] during the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]] in Japan. It was briefly revived in the early years of the Meiji government, abolished once, and later re-established with modifications in [[:en:1886|1886]]. Its successor, the [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], currently manages imperial tombs ([[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]]).

== Duties and History under the Ritsuryo System ==
The Shoryō-ryō was initially established as {{ill|Shoryoshi|ja|諸陵司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|1=Shoryō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632153}} (和訓: みささぎのつかさ) under the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryo Code]]. In [[:en:729|729]] ([[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] 6/ [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyo]] 元年), it was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Dormitory|ryo]]. Its primary duties included managing {{ill|Imperial tombs|ja|陵墓|1=Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial tombs|qq=}}, conducting {{ill|funeral rites|ja|喪葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/funeral rites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and overseeing rituals for the funerals of members of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Specifically, it managed the registries of the {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}, who were assigned to guard the tombs. In cases of misfortune, officials were sometimes sent to console the spirits of neglected Imperial Family members, as seen in the case of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]].

* {{ill|Clerk (従七位上)|ja|属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clerk (従七位上)|1=Clerk (従七位上)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124394399}}

== Meiji Government ==
In [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (Meiji 2), the Shoryō-ryō was re-established under the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] with roles such as head, assistant, clerk, and others. The head of the Shoryō-ryō at the time was {{ill|Toda Tadazumi|ja|戸田忠至|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadazumi|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}}, who also served as a {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja|宮内大丞|Ministry of the Imperial Household=Ministry of the Imperial Household|lt=Miyauchi-daiyoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193428}}.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|pages=478|url={{NDLDC|1242382/260}}}}</ref><ref name="toike">{{cite book|和書|author=外池昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2000|page=71}}</ref> During this time, the tombs of past emperors were re-evaluated, and posthumous titles were granted, such as [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Emperor Kobun]] and [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>

In [[:en:1871|Meiji 4]], the Shoryō-ryō was merged into the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Divinities]]. After several reorganizations, it was incorporated into the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] as the Shoryo Section. Later, the management was transferred to the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]], where it became the Imperial Tombs Section in 1878.<ref name=toike/>

In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]], the Shoryō-ryō was revived.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/><ref name=toike/>

Its successor, the [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], currently manages imperial tombs ([[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]]).

== Duties and History under the Ritsuryo System ==
The Shoryō-ryō was initially established as {{ill|Shoryoshi|ja|諸陵司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|1=Shoryō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632153}} (和訓: みささぎのつかさ) under the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryo Code]]. In [[:en:729|729]] ([[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] 6/ [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyo]] 元年), it was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Dormitory|ryo]]. Its primary duties included managing {{ill|Imperial tombs|ja|陵墓|1=Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial tombs|qq=}}, conducting {{ill|funeral rites|ja|喪葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/funeral rites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and overseeing rituals for the funerals of members of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Specifically, it managed the registries of the {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}, who were assigned to guard the tombs. In cases of misfortune, officials were sometimes sent to console the spirits of neglected Imperial Family members, as seen in the case of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]].

== Staff ==
* [[:en:Head|Head (従五位上)]]
* {{ill|Assistant (正六位下)|ja|助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant (正六位下)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Junior Assistant (正七位下)|ja|允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant (正七位下)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|1=ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457645}}
* {{ill|Clerk (従七位上)|ja|属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clerk (従七位上)|1=Clerk (従七位上)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124394399}}

* {{ill|Shisho|ja|史生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho|1=shishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412126}}

* {{ill|Chokutei|ja|直丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokutei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

* {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}
* {{ill|Shugo|ja|守戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夙|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夙|1=shuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430570}}

=== Staff of Shoryoshi ===
* {{ill|Chief (正六位上)|ja|正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief (正六位上)|1=正|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3594991}}
* {{ill|Deputy (従七位下)|ja|佑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deputy (従七位下)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Senior Clerk (大初位上)|ja|大令史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Clerk (大初位上)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Junior Clerk (大初位下)|ja|少令史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Clerk (大初位下)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* [[:en:Radical 32|Tsubebe]] – Ritual staff for funerals.

== Meiji Government ==
In [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (Meiji 2), the Shoryō-ryō was re-established under the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] with roles such as head, assistant, clerk, and others. The head of the Shoryō-ryō at the time was {{ill|Toda Tadazumi|ja|戸田忠至|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadazumi|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}}, who also served as a {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja|宮内大丞|Ministry of the Imperial Household=Ministry of the Imperial Household|lt=Miyauchi-daiyoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193428}}.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|pages=478|url={{NDLDC|1242382/260}}}}</ref><ref name="toike">{{cite book|和書|author=外池昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2000|page=71}}</ref> During this time, the tombs of past emperors were re-evaluated, and posthumous titles were granted, such as [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Emperor Kobun]] and [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>

In [[:en:1871|Meiji 4]], the Shoryō-ryō was merged into the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Divinities]]. After several reorganizations, it was incorporated into the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] as the Shoryo Section. Later, the management was transferred to the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]], where it became the Imperial Tombs Section in 1878.<ref name=toike/>

In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]], the Shoryō-ryō was revived.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/><ref name=toike/>

It was briefly revived in the early years of the Meiji government, abolished once, and later re-established with modifications in [[:en:1886|1886]]. Its successor, the [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], currently manages imperial tombs ([[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]]).

Wikidata name: Shoryō-ryō
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Shoryō-ryō''' (諸陵寮) was a [[:en:Ritsuryō|government office]] under the [[:en:Ministry of Ceremonies (Japan)|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] during the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]] in Japan. It was briefly revived in the early years of the Meiji government, abolished once, and later re-established with modifications in [[:en:1886|1886]]. Its successor, the [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], currently manages imperial tombs ([[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]]).

== Duties and History under the Ritsuryo System ==
The Shoryō-ryō was initially established as {{ill|Shoryoshi|ja|諸陵司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|1=Shoryō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632153}} (和訓: みささぎのつかさ) under the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryo Code]]. In [[:en:729|729]] ([[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] 6/ [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyo]] 元年), it was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Dormitory|ryo]]. Its primary duties included managing {{ill|Imperial tombs|ja|陵墓|1=Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial tombs|qq=}}, conducting {{ill|funeral rites|ja|喪葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/funeral rites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and overseeing rituals for the funerals of members of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Specifically, it managed the registries of the {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}, who were assigned to guard the tombs. In cases of misfortune, officials were sometimes sent to console the spirits of neglected Imperial Family members, as seen in the case of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]].

== Staff ==
* [[:en:Head|Head (従五位上)]]
* {{ill|Assistant (正六位下)|ja|助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant (正六位下)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Junior Assistant (正七位下)|ja|允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant (正七位下)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|1=ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457645}}
* {{ill|Clerk (従七位上)|ja|属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clerk (従七位上)|1=Clerk (従七位上)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124394399}}

* {{ill|Shisho|ja|史生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho|1=shishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412126}}
* {{ill|Shibu|ja|使部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Chokutei|ja|直丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokutei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

* {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}
* {{ill|Shugo|ja|守戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夙|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夙|1=shuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430570}}

=== Staff of Shoryoshi ===
* {{ill|Chief (正六位上)|ja|正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief (正六位上)|1=正|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3594991}}
* {{ill|Deputy (従七位下)|ja|佑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deputy (従七位下)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Senior Clerk (大初位上)|ja|大令史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Clerk (大初位上)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Junior Clerk (大初位下)|ja|少令史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Clerk (大初位下)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* [[:en:Radical 32|Tsubebe]] – Ritual staff for funerals.

== Meiji Government ==
In [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (Meiji 2), the Shoryō-ryō was re-established under the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] with roles such as head, assistant, clerk, and others. The head of the Shoryō-ryō at the time was {{ill|Toda Tadazumi|ja|戸田忠至|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadazumi|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}}, who also served as a {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja|宮内大丞|Ministry of the Imperial Household=Ministry of the Imperial Household|lt=Miyauchi-daiyoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193428}}.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|pages=478|url={{NDLDC|1242382/260}}}}</ref><ref name="toike">{{cite book|和書|author=外池昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2000|page=71}}</ref> During this time, the tombs of past emperors were re-evaluated, and posthumous titles were granted, such as [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Emperor Kobun]] and [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>

In [[:en:1871|Meiji 4]], the Shoryō-ryō was merged into the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Divinities]]. After several reorganizations, it was incorporated into the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] as the Shoryo Section. Later, the management was transferred to the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]], where it became the Imperial Tombs Section in 1878.<ref name=toike/>

In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]], the Shoryō-ryō was revived.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/><ref name=toike/>

In [[:en:1871|Meiji 4]], the Shoryō-ryō was merged into the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Divinities]]. After several reorganizations, it was incorporated into the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] as the Shoryo Section. Later, the management was transferred to the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]], where it became the Imperial Tombs Section in 1878.<ref name=toike/>

In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]], the Shoryō-ryō was revived.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/><ref name=toike/>

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11632153]]
Wikidata name: Shoryō-ryō
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Shoryō-ryō''' (諸陵寮) was a [[:en:Ritsuryō|government office]] under the [[:en:Ministry of Ceremonies (Japan)|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] during the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]] in Japan. It was briefly revived in the early years of the Meiji government, abolished once, and later re-established with modifications in [[:en:1886|1886]]. Its successor, the [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], currently manages imperial tombs ([[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]]).

== Duties and History under the Ritsuryo System ==
The Shoryō-ryō was initially established as {{ill|Shoryoshi|ja|諸陵司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|1=Shoryō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632153}} (和訓: みささぎのつかさ) under the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryo Code]]. In [[:en:729|729]] ([[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] 6/ [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyo]] 元年), it was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Dormitory|ryo]]. Its primary duties included managing {{ill|Imperial tombs|ja|陵墓|1=Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial tombs|qq=}}, conducting {{ill|funeral rites|ja|喪葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/funeral rites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and overseeing rituals for the funerals of members of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Specifically, it managed the registries of the {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}, who were assigned to guard the tombs. In cases of misfortune, officials were sometimes sent to console the spirits of neglected Imperial Family members, as seen in the case of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]].

== Staff ==
* [[:en:Head|Head (従五位上)]]
* {{ill|Assistant (正六位下)|ja|助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant (正六位下)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Junior Assistant (正七位下)|ja|允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant (正七位下)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|1=ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457645}}
* {{ill|Clerk (従七位上)|ja|属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clerk (従七位上)|1=Clerk (従七位上)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124394399}}

* {{ill|Shisho|ja|史生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho|1=shishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412126}}
* {{ill|Shibu|ja|使部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Chokutei|ja|直丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokutei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

* {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}
* {{ill|Shugo|ja|守戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夙|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夙|1=shuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430570}}

=== Staff of Shoryoshi ===

* {{ill|Deputy (従七位下)|ja|佑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deputy (従七位下)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

* {{ill|Junior Clerk (大初位下)|ja|少令史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Clerk (大初位下)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* [[:en:Radical 32|Tsubebe]] – Ritual staff for funerals.

== Meiji Government ==
In [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (Meiji 2), the Shoryō-ryō was re-established under the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] with roles such as head, assistant, clerk, and others. The head of the Shoryō-ryō at the time was {{ill|Toda Tadazumi|ja|戸田忠至|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadazumi|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}}, who also served as a {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja|宮内大丞|Ministry of the Imperial Household=Ministry of the Imperial Household|lt=Miyauchi-daiyoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193428}}.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|pages=478|url={{NDLDC|1242382/260}}}}</ref><ref name="toike">{{cite book|和書|author=外池昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2000|page=71}}</ref> During this time, the tombs of past emperors were re-evaluated, and posthumous titles were granted, such as [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Emperor Kobun]] and [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>

In [[:en:1871|Meiji 4]], the Shoryō-ryō was merged into the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Divinities]]. After several reorganizations, it was incorporated into the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] as the Shoryo Section. Later, the management was transferred to the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]], where it became the Imperial Tombs Section in 1878.<ref name=toike/>

In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]], the Shoryō-ryō was revived.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/><ref name=toike/>

* {{ill|Junior Assistant (正七位下)|ja|允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant (正七位下)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|1=ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457645}}
* {{ill|Clerk (従七位上)|ja|属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clerk (従七位上)|1=Clerk (従七位上)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124394399}}

* {{ill|Shisho|ja|史生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho|1=shishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412126}}

=== Staff of Shoryoshi ===
* {{ill|Chief (正六位上)|ja|正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief (正六位上)|1=正|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3594991}}
* {{ill|Deputy (従七位下)|ja|佑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deputy (従七位下)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Senior Clerk (大初位上)|ja|大令史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Clerk (大初位上)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Junior Clerk (大初位下)|ja|少令史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Clerk (大初位下)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* [[:en:Radical 32|Tsubebe]] – Ritual staff for funerals.

== Meiji Government ==
In [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (Meiji 2), the Shoryō-ryō was re-established under the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] with roles such as head, assistant, clerk, and others. The head of the Shoryō-ryō at the time was {{ill|Toda Tadazumi|ja|戸田忠至|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadazumi|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}}, who also served as a {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja|宮内大丞|Ministry of the Imperial Household=Ministry of the Imperial Household|lt=Miyauchi-daiyoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193428}}.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|pages=478|url={{NDLDC|1242382/260}}}}</ref><ref name="toike">{{cite book|和書|author=外池昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2000|page=71}}</ref> During this time, the tombs of past emperors were re-evaluated, and posthumous titles were granted, such as [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Emperor Kobun]] and [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>

In [[:en:1871|Meiji 4]], the Shoryō-ryō was merged into the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Divinities]]. After several reorganizations, it was incorporated into the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] as the Shoryo Section. Later, the management was transferred to the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]], where it became the Imperial Tombs Section in 1878.<ref name=toike/>

In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]], the Shoryō-ryō was revived.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/><ref name=toike/>

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11632153]]
Wikidata name: Shoryō-ryō
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Shoryō-ryō''' (諸陵寮) was a [[:en:Ritsuryō|government office]] under the [[:en:Ministry of Ceremonies (Japan)|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] during the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]] in Japan. It was briefly revived in the early years of the Meiji government, abolished once, and later re-established with modifications in [[:en:1886|1886]]. Its successor, the [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], currently manages imperial tombs ([[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]]).

== Duties and History under the Ritsuryo System ==
The Shoryō-ryō was initially established as {{ill|Shoryoshi|ja|諸陵司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|1=Shoryō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632153}} (和訓: みささぎのつかさ) under the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryo Code]]. In [[:en:729|729]] ([[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] 6/ [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyo]] 元年), it was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Dormitory|ryo]]. Its primary duties included managing {{ill|Imperial tombs|ja|陵墓|1=Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial tombs|qq=}}, conducting {{ill|funeral rites|ja|喪葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/funeral rites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and overseeing rituals for the funerals of members of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Specifically, it managed the registries of the {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}, who were assigned to guard the tombs. In cases of misfortune, officials were sometimes sent to console the spirits of neglected Imperial Family members, as seen in the case of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]].

== Staff ==
* [[:en:Head|Head (従五位上)]]

* {{ill|Clerk (従七位上)|ja|属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clerk (従七位上)|1=Clerk (従七位上)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124394399}}

* {{ill|Shisho|ja|史生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho|1=shishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412126}}
* {{ill|Shibu|ja|使部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Chokutei|ja|直丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokutei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

* {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}
* {{ill|Shugo|ja|守戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夙|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夙|1=shuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430570}}

* [[:en:Radical 32|Tsubebe]] – Ritual staff for funerals.

== Meiji Government ==
In [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (Meiji 2), the Shoryō-ryō was re-established under the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] with roles such as head, assistant, clerk, and others. The head of the Shoryō-ryō at the time was {{ill|Toda Tadazumi|ja|戸田忠至|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadazumi|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}}, who also served as a {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja|宮内大丞|Ministry of the Imperial Household=Ministry of the Imperial Household|lt=Miyauchi-daiyoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193428}}.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|pages=478|url={{NDLDC|1242382/260}}}}</ref><ref name="toike">{{cite book|和書|author=外池昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2000|page=71}}</ref> During this time, the tombs of past emperors were re-evaluated, and posthumous titles were granted, such as [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Emperor Kobun]] and [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>

In [[:en:1871|Meiji 4]], the Shoryō-ryō was merged into the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Divinities]]. After several reorganizations, it was incorporated into the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] as the Shoryo Section. Later, the management was transferred to the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]], where it became the Imperial Tombs Section in 1878.<ref name=toike/>

In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]], the Shoryō-ryō was revived.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/><ref name=toike/>

'''Shoryō-ryō''' (諸陵寮) was a [[:en:Ritsuryō|government office]] under the [[:en:Ministry of Ceremonies (Japan)|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] during the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]] in Japan. It was briefly revived in the early years of the Meiji government, abolished once, and later re-established with modifications in [[:en:1886|1886]]. Its successor, the [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], currently manages imperial tombs ([[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]]).

== Duties and History under the Ritsuryo System ==
The Shoryō-ryō was initially established as {{ill|Shoryoshi|ja|諸陵司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|1=Shoryō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632153}} (和訓: みささぎのつかさ) under the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryo Code]]. In [[:en:729|729]] ([[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] 6/ [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyo]] 元年), it was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Dormitory|ryo]]. Its primary duties included managing {{ill|Imperial tombs|ja|陵墓|1=Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial tombs|qq=}}, conducting {{ill|funeral rites|ja|喪葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/funeral rites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and overseeing rituals for the funerals of members of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Specifically, it managed the registries of the {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}, who were assigned to guard the tombs. In cases of misfortune, officials were sometimes sent to console the spirits of neglected Imperial Family members, as seen in the case of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]].

== Meiji Government ==
In [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (Meiji 2), the Shoryō-ryō was re-established under the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] with roles such as head, assistant, clerk, and others. The head of the Shoryō-ryō at the time was {{ill|Toda Tadazumi|ja|戸田忠至|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadazumi|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}}, who also served as a {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja|宮内大丞|Ministry of the Imperial Household=Ministry of the Imperial Household|lt=Miyauchi-daiyoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193428}}.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|pages=478|url={{NDLDC|1242382/260}}}}</ref><ref name="toike">{{cite book|和書|author=外池昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2000|page=71}}</ref> During this time, the tombs of past emperors were re-evaluated, and posthumous titles were granted, such as [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Emperor Kobun]] and [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>

In [[:en:1871|Meiji 4]], the Shoryō-ryō was merged into the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Divinities]]. After several reorganizations, it was incorporated into the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] as the Shoryo Section. Later, the management was transferred to the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]], where it became the Imperial Tombs Section in 1878.<ref name=toike/>

In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]], the Shoryō-ryō was revived.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/><ref name=toike/>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諸陵寮]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11632153]]
Wikidata name: Shoryō-ryō
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Shoryō-ryō''' (諸陵寮) was a [[:en:Ritsuryō|government office]] under the [[:en:Ministry of Ceremonies (Japan)|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] during the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]] in Japan. It was briefly revived in the early years of the Meiji government, abolished once, and later re-established with modifications in [[:en:1886|1886]]. Its successor, the [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], currently manages imperial tombs ([[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]]).

== Duties and History under the Ritsuryo System ==
The Shoryō-ryō was initially established as {{ill|Shoryoshi|ja|諸陵司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|1=Shoryō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632153}} (和訓: みささぎのつかさ) under the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryo Code]]. In [[:en:729|729]] ([[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] 6/ [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyo]] 元年), it was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Dormitory|ryo]]. Its primary duties included managing {{ill|Imperial tombs|ja|陵墓|1=Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial tombs|qq=}}, conducting {{ill|funeral rites|ja|喪葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/funeral rites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and overseeing rituals for the funerals of members of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Specifically, it managed the registries of the {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}, who were assigned to guard the tombs. In cases of misfortune, officials were sometimes sent to console the spirits of neglected Imperial Family members, as seen in the case of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]].

== Staff ==
* [[:en:Head|Head (従五位上)]]
* {{ill|Assistant (正六位下)|ja|助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant (正六位下)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Junior Assistant (正七位下)|ja|允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant (正七位下)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|1=ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457645}}
* {{ill|Clerk (従七位上)|ja|属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clerk (従七位上)|1=Clerk (従七位上)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124394399}}

* {{ill|Shisho|ja|史生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho|1=shishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412126}}
* {{ill|Shibu|ja|使部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Chokutei|ja|直丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokutei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

* {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}
* {{ill|Shugo|ja|守戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夙|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夙|1=shuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430570}}

=== Staff of Shoryoshi ===
* {{ill|Chief (正六位上)|ja|正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief (正六位上)|1=正|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3594991}}
* {{ill|Deputy (従七位下)|ja|佑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deputy (従七位下)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Senior Clerk (大初位上)|ja|大令史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Clerk (大初位上)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Junior Clerk (大初位下)|ja|少令史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Clerk (大初位下)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* [[:en:Radical 32|Tsubebe]] – Ritual staff for funerals.

== Meiji Government ==
In [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (Meiji 2), the Shoryō-ryō was re-established under the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] with roles such as head, assistant, clerk, and others. The head of the Shoryō-ryō at the time was {{ill|Toda Tadazumi|ja|戸田忠至|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadazumi|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}}, who also served as a {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja|宮内大丞|Ministry of the Imperial Household=Ministry of the Imperial Household|lt=Miyauchi-daiyoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193428}}.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|pages=478|url={{NDLDC|1242382/260}}}}</ref><ref name="toike">{{cite book|和書|author=外池昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2000|page=71}}</ref> During this time, the tombs of past emperors were re-evaluated, and posthumous titles were granted, such as [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Emperor Kobun]] and [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>

In [[:en:1871|Meiji 4]], the Shoryō-ryō was merged into the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Divinities]]. After several reorganizations, it was incorporated into the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] as the Shoryo Section. Later, the management was transferred to the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]], where it became the Imperial Tombs Section in 1878.<ref name=toike/>

In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]], the Shoryō-ryō was revived.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/><ref name=toike/>

== Meiji Government ==
In [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (Meiji 2), the Shoryō-ryō was re-established under the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] with roles such as head, assistant, clerk, and others. The head of the Shoryō-ryō at the time was {{ill|Toda Tadazumi|ja|戸田忠至|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadazumi|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}}, who also served as a {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja|宮内大丞|Ministry of the Imperial Household=Ministry of the Imperial Household|lt=Miyauchi-daiyoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193428}}.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|pages=478|url={{NDLDC|1242382/260}}}}</ref><ref name="toike">{{cite book|和書|author=外池昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2000|page=71}}</ref> During this time, the tombs of past emperors were re-evaluated, and posthumous titles were granted, such as [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Emperor Kobun]] and [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>

In [[:en:1871|Meiji 4]], the Shoryō-ryō was merged into the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Divinities]]. After several reorganizations, it was incorporated into the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] as the Shoryo Section. Later, the management was transferred to the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]], where it became the Imperial Tombs Section in 1878.<ref name=toike/>

In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]], the Shoryō-ryō was revived.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/><ref name=toike/>

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11632153]]
Wikidata name: Shoryō-ryō
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Shoryō-ryō''' (諸陵寮) was a [[:en:Ritsuryō|government office]] under the [[:en:Ministry of Ceremonies (Japan)|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] during the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]] in Japan. It was briefly revived in the early years of the Meiji government, abolished once, and later re-established with modifications in [[:en:1886|1886]]. Its successor, the [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], currently manages imperial tombs ([[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]]).

== Duties and History under the Ritsuryo System ==
The Shoryō-ryō was initially established as {{ill|Shoryoshi|ja|諸陵司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|1=Shoryō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632153}} (和訓: みささぎのつかさ) under the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryo Code]]. In [[:en:729|729]] ([[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] 6/ [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyo]] 元年), it was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Dormitory|ryo]]. Its primary duties included managing {{ill|Imperial tombs|ja|陵墓|1=Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial tombs|qq=}}, conducting {{ill|funeral rites|ja|喪葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/funeral rites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and overseeing rituals for the funerals of members of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Specifically, it managed the registries of the {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}, who were assigned to guard the tombs. In cases of misfortune, officials were sometimes sent to console the spirits of neglected Imperial Family members, as seen in the case of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]].

* {{ill|Junior Assistant (正七位下)|ja|允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant (正七位下)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|1=ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457645}}
* {{ill|Clerk (従七位上)|ja|属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clerk (従七位上)|1=Clerk (従七位上)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124394399}}

* {{ill|Shisho|ja|史生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho|1=shishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412126}}
* {{ill|Shibu|ja|使部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Chokutei|ja|直丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokutei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

* {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}
* {{ill|Shugo|ja|守戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夙|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夙|1=shuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430570}}

=== Staff of Shoryoshi ===

* [[:en:Radical 32|Tsubebe]] – Ritual staff for funerals.

== Meiji Government ==

In [[:en:1871|Meiji 4]], the Shoryō-ryō was merged into the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Divinities]]. After several reorganizations, it was incorporated into the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] as the Shoryo Section. Later, the management was transferred to the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]], where it became the Imperial Tombs Section in 1878.<ref name=toike/>

* {{ill|Shibu|ja|使部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Chokutei|ja|直丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokutei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

* {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}

=== Staff of Shoryoshi ===
* {{ill|Chief (正六位上)|ja|正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief (正六位上)|1=正|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3594991}}
* {{ill|Deputy (従七位下)|ja|佑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deputy (従七位下)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Senior Clerk (大初位上)|ja|大令史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Clerk (大初位上)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Junior Clerk (大初位下)|ja|少令史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Clerk (大初位下)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* [[:en:Radical 32|Tsubebe]] – Ritual staff for funerals.

== Meiji Government ==
In [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (Meiji 2), the Shoryō-ryō was re-established under the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] with roles such as head, assistant, clerk, and others. The head of the Shoryō-ryō at the time was {{ill|Toda Tadazumi|ja|戸田忠至|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadazumi|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}}, who also served as a {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja|宮内大丞|Ministry of the Imperial Household=Ministry of the Imperial Household|lt=Miyauchi-daiyoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193428}}.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|pages=478|url={{NDLDC|1242382/260}}}}</ref><ref name="toike">{{cite book|和書|author=外池昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2000|page=71}}</ref> During this time, the tombs of past emperors were re-evaluated, and posthumous titles were granted, such as [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Emperor Kobun]] and [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>

In [[:en:1871|Meiji 4]], the Shoryō-ryō was merged into the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Divinities]]. After several reorganizations, it was incorporated into the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] as the Shoryo Section. Later, the management was transferred to the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]], where it became the Imperial Tombs Section in 1878.<ref name=toike/>

In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]], the Shoryō-ryō was revived.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/><ref name=toike/>
