{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11632153]]
Wikidata name: Shoryō-ryō
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Shoryō-ryō''' (諸陵寮) was a [[:en:Ritsuryō|government office]] under the [[:en:Ministry of Ceremonies (Japan)|Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs]] during the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]] in Japan. It was briefly revived in the early years of the Meiji government, abolished once, and later re-established with modifications in [[:en:1886|1886]]. Its successor, the [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Archives and Mausolea Department]], currently manages imperial tombs ([[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]]).

== Duties and History under the Ritsuryo System ==
The Shoryō-ryō was initially established as {{ill|Shoryoshi|ja|諸陵司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|1=Shoryō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632153}} (和訓: みささぎのつかさ) under the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryo Code]]. In [[:en:729|729]] ([[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] 6/ [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyo]] 元年), it was elevated to the status of a [[:en:Dormitory|ryo]]. Its primary duties included managing {{ill|Imperial tombs|ja|陵墓|1=Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial tombs|qq=}}, conducting {{ill|funeral rites|ja|喪葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/funeral rites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and overseeing rituals for the funerals of members of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Specifically, it managed the registries of the {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}, who were assigned to guard the tombs. In cases of misfortune, officials were sometimes sent to console the spirits of neglected Imperial Family members, as seen in the case of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]].

== Staff ==
* [[:en:Head|Head (従五位上)]]
* {{ill|Assistant (正六位下)|ja|助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant (正六位下)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Junior Assistant (正七位下)|ja|允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant (正七位下)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|1=ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457645}}
* {{ill|Clerk (従七位上)|ja|属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clerk (従七位上)|1=Clerk (従七位上)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124394399}}

* {{ill|Shisho|ja|史生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho|1=shishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412126}}

* {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}
* {{ill|Shugo|ja|守戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夙|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夙|1=shuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430570}}

=== Staff of Shoryoshi ===

* [[:en:Radical 32|Tsubebe]] – Ritual staff for funerals.

== Meiji Government ==
In [[:en:1869|Meiji 2]] (Meiji 2), the Shoryō-ryō was re-established under the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] with roles such as head, assistant, clerk, and others. The head of the Shoryō-ryō at the time was {{ill|Toda Tadazumi|ja|戸田忠至|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadazumi|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}}, who also served as a {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja|宮内大丞|Ministry of the Imperial Household=Ministry of the Imperial Household|lt=Miyauchi-daiyoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193428}}.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|pages=478|url={{NDLDC|1242382/260}}}}</ref><ref name="toike">{{cite book|和書|author=外池昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2000|page=71}}</ref> During this time, the tombs of past emperors were re-evaluated, and posthumous titles were granted, such as [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Emperor Kobun]] and [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>

In [[:en:1871|Meiji 4]], the Shoryō-ryō was merged into the [[:en:Ministry of Divinities|Ministry of Divinities]]. After several reorganizations, it was incorporated into the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] as the Shoryo Section. Later, the management was transferred to the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]], where it became the Imperial Tombs Section in 1878.<ref name=toike/>

In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]], the Shoryō-ryō was revived.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/><ref name=toike/>

It was briefly revived in the early years of the Meiji government, abolished once, and later re-established with modifications in {{ill|1886|en|1886|WD=Q7822|lt=1886}}. Its successor, the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|en|Archives and Mausolea Department|WD=Q4346533|lt=Archives and Mausolea Department}}, currently manages imperial tombs ({{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|en|Japanese imperial tombs|WD=Q17218816|lt=Japanese imperial tombs}}).

== Duties and History under the Ritsuryo System ==
The Shoryō-ryō was initially established as {{ill|Shoryoshi|ja|諸陵司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|1=Shoryō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632153}} (和訓: みささぎのつかさ) under the {{ill|Traditional Chinese law|en|Traditional Chinese law|WD=Q4380197|lt=Ritsuryo Code}}. In {{ill|729|en|729|WD=Q23561|lt=729}} ({{ill|Jinki (era)|en|Jinki (era)|WD=Q1373210|lt=Shinki}} 6/ {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyo}} 元年), it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Dormitory|en|Dormitory|WD=Q847950|lt=ryo}}. Its primary duties included managing {{ill|Imperial tombs|ja|陵墓|1=Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial tombs|qq=}}, conducting {{ill|funeral rites|ja|喪葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/funeral rites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and overseeing rituals for the funerals of members of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Specifically, it managed the registries of the {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}, who were assigned to guard the tombs. In cases of misfortune, officials were sometimes sent to console the spirits of neglected Imperial Family members, as seen in the case of {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara}}.

In {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2}} (Meiji 2), the Shoryō-ryō was re-established under the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities}} with roles such as head, assistant, clerk, and others. The head of the Shoryō-ryō at the time was {{ill|Toda Tadazumi|ja|戸田忠至|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadazumi|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}}, who also served as a {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja|宮内大丞|Ministry of the Imperial Household=Ministry of the Imperial Household|lt=Miyauchi-daiyoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193428}}.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|pages=478|url={{NDLDC|1242382/260}}}}</ref><ref name="toike">{{cite book|和書|author=外池昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2000|page=71}}</ref> During this time, the tombs of past emperors were re-evaluated, and posthumous titles were granted, such as {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|en|Emperor Kōbun|WD=Q357242|lt=Emperor Kobun}} and {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin}}.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>

In {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=Meiji 4}}, the Shoryō-ryō was merged into the {{ill|Ministry of Divinities|en|Ministry of Divinities|WD=Q4294696|lt=Ministry of Divinities}}. After several reorganizations, it was incorporated into the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion}} as the Shoryo Section. Later, the management was transferred to the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|WD=Q8193428|lt=Imperial Household Ministry}}, where it became the Imperial Tombs Section in 1878.<ref name=toike/>

In {{ill|1886|en|1886|WD=Q7822|lt=Meiji 19}}, the Shoryō-ryō was revived.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/><ref name=toike/>

'''Shoryō-ryō''' (諸陵寮) was a {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=government office}} under the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonies (Japan)|en|Ministry of Ceremonies (Japan)|WD=Q10829855|lt=Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs}} during the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryo system}} in Japan. It was briefly revived in the early years of the Meiji government, abolished once, and later re-established with modifications in {{ill|1886|en|1886|WD=Q7822|lt=1886}}. Its successor, the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|en|Archives and Mausolea Department|WD=Q4346533|lt=Archives and Mausolea Department}}, currently manages imperial tombs ({{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|en|Japanese imperial tombs|WD=Q17218816|lt=Japanese imperial tombs}}).

== Duties and History under the Ritsuryo System ==
The Shoryō-ryō was initially established as {{ill|Shoryoshi|ja|諸陵司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|1=Shoryō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632153}} (和訓: みささぎのつかさ) under the {{ill|Traditional Chinese law|en|Traditional Chinese law|WD=Q4380197|lt=Ritsuryo Code}}. In {{ill|729|en|729|WD=Q23561|lt=729}} ({{ill|Jinki (era)|en|Jinki (era)|WD=Q1373210|lt=Shinki}} 6/ {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyo}} 元年), it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Dormitory|en|Dormitory|WD=Q847950|lt=ryo}}. Its primary duties included managing {{ill|Imperial tombs|ja|陵墓|1=Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial tombs|qq=}}, conducting {{ill|funeral rites|ja|喪葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/funeral rites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and overseeing rituals for the funerals of members of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Specifically, it managed the registries of the {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}, who were assigned to guard the tombs. In cases of misfortune, officials were sometimes sent to console the spirits of neglected Imperial Family members, as seen in the case of {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara}}.

== Staff ==
* {{ill|Head|en|Head|WD=Q23640|lt=Head (従五位上)}}
* {{ill|Assistant (正六位下)|ja|助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant (正六位下)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Junior Assistant (正七位下)|ja|允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant (正七位下)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|1=ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457645}}
* {{ill|Clerk (従七位上)|ja|属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clerk (従七位上)|1=Clerk (従七位上)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124394399}}

* {{ill|Shisho|ja|史生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho|1=shishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412126}}
* {{ill|Shibu|ja|使部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Chokutei|ja|直丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokutei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

* {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}
* {{ill|Shugo|ja|守戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夙|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夙|1=shuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430570}}

=== Staff of Shoryoshi ===
* {{ill|Chief (正六位上)|ja|正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief (正六位上)|1=正|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3594991}}
* {{ill|Deputy (従七位下)|ja|佑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deputy (従七位下)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Senior Clerk (大初位上)|ja|大令史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Clerk (大初位上)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Junior Clerk (大初位下)|ja|少令史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Clerk (大初位下)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Radical 32|en|Radical 32|WD=Q95507|lt=Tsubebe}} – Ritual staff for funerals.

== Meiji Government ==
In {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2}} (Meiji 2), the Shoryō-ryō was re-established under the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities}} with roles such as head, assistant, clerk, and others. The head of the Shoryō-ryō at the time was {{ill|Toda Tadazumi|ja|戸田忠至|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadazumi|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}}, who also served as a {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja|宮内大丞|Ministry of the Imperial Household=Ministry of the Imperial Household|lt=Miyauchi-daiyoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193428}}.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|pages=478|url={{NDLDC|1242382/260}}}}</ref><ref name="toike">{{cite book|和書|author=外池昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2000|page=71}}</ref> During this time, the tombs of past emperors were re-evaluated, and posthumous titles were granted, such as {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|en|Emperor Kōbun|WD=Q357242|lt=Emperor Kobun}} and {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin}}.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>

In {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=Meiji 4}}, the Shoryō-ryō was merged into the {{ill|Ministry of Divinities|en|Ministry of Divinities|WD=Q4294696|lt=Ministry of Divinities}}. After several reorganizations, it was incorporated into the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion}} as the Shoryo Section. Later, the management was transferred to the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|WD=Q8193428|lt=Imperial Household Ministry}}, where it became the Imperial Tombs Section in 1878.<ref name=toike/>

In {{ill|1886|en|1886|WD=Q7822|lt=Meiji 19}}, the Shoryō-ryō was revived.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/><ref name=toike/>

== Meiji Government ==
In {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2}} (Meiji 2), the Shoryō-ryō was re-established under the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities}} with roles such as head, assistant, clerk, and others. The head of the Shoryō-ryō at the time was {{ill|Toda Tadazumi|ja|戸田忠至|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadazumi|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}}, who also served as a {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja|宮内大丞|Ministry of the Imperial Household=Ministry of the Imperial Household|lt=Miyauchi-daiyoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193428}}.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|pages=478|url={{NDLDC|1242382/260}}}}</ref><ref name="toike">{{cite book|和書|author=外池昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2000|page=71}}</ref> During this time, the tombs of past emperors were re-evaluated, and posthumous titles were granted, such as {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|en|Emperor Kōbun|WD=Q357242|lt=Emperor Kobun}} and {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin}}.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>

In {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=Meiji 4}}, the Shoryō-ryō was merged into the {{ill|Ministry of Divinities|en|Ministry of Divinities|WD=Q4294696|lt=Ministry of Divinities}}. After several reorganizations, it was incorporated into the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion}} as the Shoryo Section. Later, the management was transferred to the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|WD=Q8193428|lt=Imperial Household Ministry}}, where it became the Imperial Tombs Section in 1878.<ref name=toike/>

In {{ill|1886|en|1886|WD=Q7822|lt=Meiji 19}}, the Shoryō-ryō was revived.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/><ref name=toike/>

'''Shoryō-ryō''' (諸陵寮) was a {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=government office}} under the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonies (Japan)|en|Ministry of Ceremonies (Japan)|WD=Q10829855|lt=Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs}} during the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryo system}} in Japan. It was briefly revived in the early years of the Meiji government, abolished once, and later re-established with modifications in {{ill|1886|en|1886|WD=Q7822|lt=1886}}. Its successor, the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|en|Archives and Mausolea Department|WD=Q4346533|lt=Archives and Mausolea Department}}, currently manages imperial tombs ({{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|en|Japanese imperial tombs|WD=Q17218816|lt=Japanese imperial tombs}}).

== Duties and History under the Ritsuryo System ==
The Shoryō-ryō was initially established as {{ill|Shoryoshi|ja|諸陵司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|1=Shoryō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632153}} (和訓: みささぎのつかさ) under the {{ill|Traditional Chinese law|en|Traditional Chinese law|WD=Q4380197|lt=Ritsuryo Code}}. In {{ill|729|en|729|WD=Q23561|lt=729}} ({{ill|Jinki (era)|en|Jinki (era)|WD=Q1373210|lt=Shinki}} 6/ {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyo}} 元年), it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Dormitory|en|Dormitory|WD=Q847950|lt=ryo}}. Its primary duties included managing {{ill|Imperial tombs|ja|陵墓|1=Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial tombs|qq=}}, conducting {{ill|funeral rites|ja|喪葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/funeral rites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and overseeing rituals for the funerals of members of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Specifically, it managed the registries of the {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}, who were assigned to guard the tombs. In cases of misfortune, officials were sometimes sent to console the spirits of neglected Imperial Family members, as seen in the case of {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara}}.

== Staff ==
* {{ill|Head|en|Head|WD=Q23640|lt=Head (従五位上)}}
* {{ill|Assistant (正六位下)|ja|助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant (正六位下)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Junior Assistant (正七位下)|ja|允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant (正七位下)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寮 (律令制)|1=ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457645}}
* {{ill|Clerk (従七位上)|ja|属|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clerk (従七位上)|1=Clerk (従七位上)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124394399}}

* {{ill|Shisho|ja|史生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho|1=shishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412126}}
* {{ill|Shibu|ja|使部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Chokutei|ja|直丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokutei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

* {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}
* {{ill|Shugo|ja|守戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夙|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夙|1=shuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430570}}

=== Staff of Shoryoshi ===
* {{ill|Chief (正六位上)|ja|正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief (正六位上)|1=正|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3594991}}
* {{ill|Deputy (従七位下)|ja|佑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deputy (従七位下)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Senior Clerk (大初位上)|ja|大令史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Clerk (大初位上)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Junior Clerk (大初位下)|ja|少令史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Clerk (大初位下)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Radical 32|en|Radical 32|WD=Q95507|lt=Tsubebe}} – Ritual staff for funerals.

== Meiji Government ==
In {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2}} (Meiji 2), the Shoryō-ryō was re-established under the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities}} with roles such as head, assistant, clerk, and others. The head of the Shoryō-ryō at the time was {{ill|Toda Tadazumi|ja|戸田忠至|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadazumi|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}}, who also served as a {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja|宮内大丞|Ministry of the Imperial Household=Ministry of the Imperial Household|lt=Miyauchi-daiyoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193428}}.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|pages=478|url={{NDLDC|1242382/260}}}}</ref><ref name="toike">{{cite book|和書|author=外池昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2000|page=71}}</ref> During this time, the tombs of past emperors were re-evaluated, and posthumous titles were granted, such as {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|en|Emperor Kōbun|WD=Q357242|lt=Emperor Kobun}} and {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin}}.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>

In {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=Meiji 4}}, the Shoryō-ryō was merged into the {{ill|Ministry of Divinities|en|Ministry of Divinities|WD=Q4294696|lt=Ministry of Divinities}}. After several reorganizations, it was incorporated into the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion}} as the Shoryo Section. Later, the management was transferred to the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|WD=Q8193428|lt=Imperial Household Ministry}}, where it became the Imperial Tombs Section in 1878.<ref name=toike/>

In {{ill|1886|en|1886|WD=Q7822|lt=Meiji 19}}, the Shoryō-ryō was revived.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/><ref name=toike/>

Its successor, the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|en|Archives and Mausolea Department|WD=Q4346533|lt=Archives and Mausolea Department|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}, currently manages imperial tombs ({{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|en|Japanese imperial tombs|WD=Q17218816|lt=Japanese imperial tombs|1=Japanese imperial tombs}}).

== Duties and History under the Ritsuryo System ==
The Shoryō-ryō was initially established as {{ill|Shoryoshi|ja|諸陵司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|1=Shoryō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632153}} (和訓: みささぎのつかさ) under the {{ill|Traditional Chinese law|en|Traditional Chinese law|WD=Q4380197|lt=Ritsuryo Code|1=Traditional Chinese law}}. In {{ill|729|en|729|WD=Q23561|lt=729|1=729}} ({{ill|Jinki (era)|en|Jinki (era)|WD=Q1373210|lt=Shinki|1=Jinki (era)}} 6/ {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyo|1=Tenpyō}} 元年), it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Dormitory|en|Dormitory|WD=Q847950|lt=ryo|1=Dormitory}}. Its primary duties included managing {{ill|Imperial tombs|ja|陵墓|1=Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial tombs|qq=}}, conducting {{ill|funeral rites|ja|喪葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/funeral rites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and overseeing rituals for the funerals of members of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Specifically, it managed the registries of the {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}, who were assigned to guard the tombs. In cases of misfortune, officials were sometimes sent to console the spirits of neglected Imperial Family members, as seen in the case of {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}}.

== Meiji Government ==
In {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2|1=1869}} (Meiji 2), the Shoryō-ryō was re-established under the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Department of Divinities}} with roles such as head, assistant, clerk, and others. The head of the Shoryō-ryō at the time was {{ill|Toda Tadazumi|ja|戸田忠至|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadazumi|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}}, who also served as a {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja|宮内大丞|Ministry of the Imperial Household=Ministry of the Imperial Household|lt=Miyauchi-daiyoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193428}}.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|pages=478|url={{NDLDC|1242382/260}}}}</ref><ref name="toike">{{cite book|和書|author=外池昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2000|page=71}}</ref> During this time, the tombs of past emperors were re-evaluated, and posthumous titles were granted, such as {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|en|Emperor Kōbun|WD=Q357242|lt=Emperor Kobun|1=Emperor Kōbun}} and {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin|1=Emperor Junnin}}.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>

In {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=Meiji 4|1=1871}}, the Shoryō-ryō was merged into the {{ill|Ministry of Divinities|en|Ministry of Divinities|WD=Q4294696|lt=Ministry of Divinities|1=Ministry of Divinities}}. After several reorganizations, it was incorporated into the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} as the Shoryo Section. Later, the management was transferred to the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|WD=Q8193428|lt=Imperial Household Ministry|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household}}, where it became the Imperial Tombs Section in 1878.<ref name=toike/>

In {{ill|1886|en|1886|WD=Q7822|lt=Meiji 19|1=1886}}, the Shoryō-ryō was revived.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/><ref name=toike/>

'''Shoryō-ryō''' (諸陵寮) was a {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=government office|1=Ritsuryō}} under the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonies (Japan)|en|Ministry of Ceremonies (Japan)|WD=Q10829855|lt=Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs|1=Ministry of Ceremonies (Japan)}} during the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryo system|1=Ritsuryō}} in Japan. It was briefly revived in the early years of the Meiji government, abolished once, and later re-established with modifications in {{ill|1886|en|1886|WD=Q7822|lt=1886|1=1886}}. Its successor, the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|en|Archives and Mausolea Department|WD=Q4346533|lt=Archives and Mausolea Department|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}, currently manages imperial tombs ({{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|en|Japanese imperial tombs|WD=Q17218816|lt=Japanese imperial tombs|1=Japanese imperial tombs}}).

== Duties and History under the Ritsuryo System ==
The Shoryō-ryō was initially established as {{ill|Shoryoshi|ja|諸陵司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸陵寮|1=Shoryō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632153}} (和訓: みささぎのつかさ) under the {{ill|Traditional Chinese law|en|Traditional Chinese law|WD=Q4380197|lt=Ritsuryo Code|1=Traditional Chinese law}}. In {{ill|729|en|729|WD=Q23561|lt=729|1=729}} ({{ill|Jinki (era)|en|Jinki (era)|WD=Q1373210|lt=Shinki|1=Jinki (era)}} 6/ {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyo|1=Tenpyō}} 元年), it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Dormitory|en|Dormitory|WD=Q847950|lt=ryo|1=Dormitory}}. Its primary duties included managing {{ill|Imperial tombs|ja|陵墓|1=Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial tombs|qq=}}, conducting {{ill|funeral rites|ja|喪葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/funeral rites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and overseeing rituals for the funerals of members of the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. Specifically, it managed the registries of the {{ill|Ryoho|ja|陵戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryoho|1=ryōko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655865}}, who were assigned to guard the tombs. In cases of misfortune, officials were sometimes sent to console the spirits of neglected Imperial Family members, as seen in the case of {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}}.

== Meiji Government ==
In {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=Meiji 2|1=1869}} (Meiji 2), the Shoryō-ryō was re-established under the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Department of Divinities}} with roles such as head, assistant, clerk, and others. The head of the Shoryō-ryō at the time was {{ill|Toda Tadazumi|ja|戸田忠至|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadazumi|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}}, who also served as a {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja|宮内大丞|Ministry of the Imperial Household=Ministry of the Imperial Household|lt=Miyauchi-daiyoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193428}}.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478>{{cite book|author=文部省維新史料編纂事務局|title=維新史|volume=5|year=1941|pages=478|url={{NDLDC|1242382/260}}}}</ref><ref name="toike">{{cite book|和書|author=外池昇|title=天皇陵の近代史|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2000|page=71}}</ref> During this time, the tombs of past emperors were re-evaluated, and posthumous titles were granted, such as {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|en|Emperor Kōbun|WD=Q357242|lt=Emperor Kobun|1=Emperor Kōbun}} and {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin|1=Emperor Junnin}}.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/>

In {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=Meiji 4|1=1871}}, the Shoryō-ryō was merged into the {{ill|Ministry of Divinities|en|Ministry of Divinities|WD=Q4294696|lt=Ministry of Divinities|1=Ministry of Divinities}}. After several reorganizations, it was incorporated into the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} as the Shoryo Section. Later, the management was transferred to the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|WD=Q8193428|lt=Imperial Household Ministry|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household}}, where it became the Imperial Tombs Section in 1878.<ref name=toike/>

In {{ill|1886|en|1886|WD=Q7822|lt=Meiji 19|1=1886}}, the Shoryō-ryō was revived.<ref name=ishinshi-5-478/><ref name=toike/>
